title: How did capitalism happen?
description: Six entries that trace the path from the first ledger to the modern economy.

entries.note: Start here. Debt is older than money — the ledger came before the coin.
Money was invented to settle debts, not to enable trade.
How the logic of exchange became the organizing principle of civilization.
The moment capital began consuming labor at scale.
The man who read capitalism most carefully and predicted its end.
The system that emerged from all of the above — and what it actually is.

title: How democracy was built
description: Six entries across 2,500 years — from the first assembly to the modern constitution.

entries.note: The first experiment. More radical than anything that followed.
How Athens became Rome — and what was lost in translation.
The ideas that made modern democracy thinkable.
The moment those ideas met reality — violently.
What the concept actually means, and why it's still contested.
The technology that tries to make democracy permanent.

title: How disease shaped civilization
description: Six entries on the organisms and epidemics that redirected human history.

entries.note: The insect that shaped where civilization could and couldn't go.
The disease that made European conquest of the Americas possible.
The plague that killed a third of Europe and ended the medieval world.
The epidemic that invented epidemiology and the modern city.
The idea that changed everything — and took decades to be believed.
What civilization built once it understood what was killing it.
